Detective Miller stared at the file on his desk. He knew the supernatural sound was fading away fast. His job relied entirely on hearing the truth. Now, he turned to the suspect sitting opposite him.

Every voice had a specific thought hidden inside it. The man whispered lies into the dark room. Yet, Miller heard the guilt beneath the words. He needed ten seconds of clarity for each clue.

Ten seconds felt like an eternity during the trial. Memories flashed before he could stop his mind. The witness denied knowing the killer personally. Miller felt the fear rise in his throat.

Suddenly, the clock ticked down to nothingness. Silence filled the courtroom abruptly. He waited for the silence to fade quickly.

He grabbed his notes from the table beside him. The evidence was clear enough for conviction. Finally, he stepped away from the case quietly.<|im_end|>
